title: Why Analysts Remain Divided On AMC's Recovery Story
text: Analysts remain divided on AMC Entertainment's recovery story, with different Wall Street firms taking contrasting positions on the stock. Citi maintained a Sell rating on AMC Entertainment Holdings while raising its price target to $1.20 from $1.10 on May 7, 2026. Meanwhile, Benchmark upgraded AMC Entertainment Holdings to Buy from Hold and assigned a $2.50 price target, citing improving box office conditions and structurally stronger per-patron economics. The two firms have notably different outlooks on the stock. While Citi remains bearish with its $1.20 target and Sell rating, Benchmark is more bullish with its $2.50 target and Buy rating, reflecting disagreements among analysts about AMC's recovery prospects in the theatrical exhibition business. The divergence in analyst opinion reflects broader uncertainty about whether the box office recovery will be strong enough to justify the company's debt levels and earn a path back to profitability.
